error_signature: permission denied to read privatekey on start
title: Permission denied for private key in Docker setup
symptom: When starting n8n in Docker, the error 'permission denied to read privatekey on start' occurs, preventing the application from accessing the SSL private key.
root_cause: The Docker container does not have the necessary permissions to access the private key file due to the way file permissions are set on the host system. This is common when using Certbot certificates that require root access to read.
fix_steps: Set up a reverse proxy (like Nginx) on the host to handle SSL termination.
Configure Nginx to read the SSL certificates and proxy requests to the n8n container over HTTP.
Ensure the Nginx configuration includes the correct paths to the SSL certificates.
Update the n8n environment variables to use HTTPS for communication with the proxy.
agent_instruction: Set up Nginx as a reverse proxy to handle SSL and configure n8n to connect via HTTPS.
